── tmp/csc/clones/gemini-2.5-pro/tmp-restructure-1773368577/.trash/repo/packages/csc-service/csc_service/server/server_s2s.py ──
# Server-to-Server (S2S) linking protocol for CSC IRC server federation.
  S2S_CRLF = b'\r\n'
  S2S_MAX_LINE = 8192
  class ServerLink:  # Represents a single UDP connection to a peer CSC server with DH encryption.
    def __init__(self, local_server, remote_host, remote_port, password, remote_server_id=None, sock=None)  # Initialize a server link.
    def connect(self)  # Establish UDP connection to remote server and initiate DH key exchange.
    def _initiate_dh_exchange(self)  # Send CRYPTOINIT DH message to initiate key exchange.
    def _handle_dh_reply(self, pubkey_hex)  # Process CRYPTOINIT DHREPLY message and derive AES key.
    def _send_dh_reply(self, pubkey_hex)  # Process incoming CRYPTOINIT DH and send DHREPLY with AES derivation.
    def authenticate(self)  # Exchange SLINK/SLINKACK handshake after DH key exchange.
    def handle_inbound_handshake(self)  # Handle authentication for an inbound (accepted) connection.
    def start_reader(self, callback)  # Start a background thread that reads S2S messages and calls callback.
    def _reader_loop(self, callback)  # Background loop reading UDP datagrams and processing S2S messages.
    def send_message(self, command, *args)  # Send an S2S command to the remote server (encrypted if key available).
    def send_raw(self, line)  # Send a raw line to the remote server (encrypted if key available).
    def is_connected(self)  # Check if this link is alive and authenticated.
    def close(self)  # Gracefully close the link.
    def _recv_line(self, timeout=None)  # Read one CRLF-terminated line from the socket.
    def _get_local_server_id(self)  # Return the local server's unique ID.
    def _log(self, message)  # Log via the local server's logger.
  class ServerNetwork:  # Manages all linked servers and network-wide operations.
    def __init__(self, local_server)  # Initialize the server network manager.
    def start_listener(self)  # Start the UDP listener for inbound S2S connections with DH encryption.
    def _receive_loop(self)  # Receive inbound S2S UDP datagrams from peers with DH and SLINK auth.
    def _handle_inbound_link(self, link)  # Handle a newly established inbound UDP S2S link.
    def link_to(self, host, port, password=None)  # Initiate an outbound link to a remote server.
    def get_peer_servers(self)  # List all connected peer server IDs.
    def get_link(self, server_id)  # Get the ServerLink for a specific peer.
    def broadcast_to_network(self, command, args_str='', exclude_server=None)  # Send an S2S command to all connected peers.
    def get_user_from_network(self, nick)  # Find a user on any server in the network.
    def get_channel_from_network(self, channel)  # Find a channel on any server in the network.
    def route_message(self, source_nick, target, text, exclude_server=None)  # Route a PRIVMSG/NOTICE across the network.
    def sync_line(self, target_nick, line, exclude_server=None)  # Route a raw IRC line to a specific user on the network.
    def sync_user_join(self, nick, host, modes, channel=None, exclude_server=None)  # Notify the network that a user joined/connected.
    def sync_user_quit(self, nick, reason='', exclude_server=None)  # Notify the network that a user disconnected.
    def sync_user_part(self, nick, channel, reason='', exclude_server=None)  # Notify the network that a user parted a channel.
    def sync_nick_change(self, old_nick, new_nick, exclude_server=None)  # Notify the network that a user changed their nickname.
    def sync_channel(self, channel_name, modes_str, members_json, exclude_server=None)  # Broadcast channel state to the network.
    def sync_topic(self, channel_name, topic, exclude_server=None)  # Broadcast channel topic to the network.
    def sync_channel_state(self, chan_name, exclude_server=None)  # Helper to sync current state of a local channel to the network.
    def _send_full_sync(self, link)  # Send complete local state to a newly linked peer.
    def _dispatch_s2s_message(self, link, command, rest)  # Route an incoming S2S command to the appropriate handler.
    def _handle_syncuser(self, link, rest)  # Handle SYNCUSER: add/update a remote user in tracking.
    def _handle_synpart(self, link, rest)  # Handle SYNPART: remote user leaves a channel.
    def _handle_syncnick(self, link, rest)  # Handle SYNCNICK: remote user changes nickname.
    def _handle_syncchan(self, link, rest)  # Handle SYNCCHAN: merge remote channel state.
    def _handle_synctopic(self, link, rest)  # Handle SYNCTOPIC: remote channel topic update.
    def _handle_syncmsg(self, link, rest)  # Handle SYNCMSG: deliver a message from the network locally.
    def _handle_syncline(self, link, rest)  # Handle SYNCLINE: deliver a raw IRC line to a local user.
    def _handle_desync(self, link, rest)  # Handle DESYNC: remove a nick or channel from remote tracking.
    def _handle_squit(self, link, rest)  # Handle SQUIT: a server is disconnecting.
    def _handle_error(self, link, rest)  # Handle ERROR message from a peer.
    def _handle_link_lost(self, link)  # Called when a link's reader thread detects a disconnect.
    def _remove_server_state(self, server_id)  # Remove all remote state belonging to a disconnected server.
    def _rename_local_user(self, old_nick, new_nick)  # Rename a local user due to nick collision.
    def shutdown(self)  # Shut down all S2S connections and the listener.
    def _get_local_server_id(self)  # Return the local server's unique ID.
    def _log(self, message)  # Log via the local server's logger.

# Channel and ChannelManager for IRC-style channel support.

── tmp/csc/clones/gemini-2.5-pro/tmp-restructure-1773368577/.trash/repo/packages/csc-service/csc_service/shared/crypto.py ──
# Diffie-Hellman key exchange and AES-256-GCM encryption for the CSC translator.
  DH_PRIME = int('FFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFC90FDAA22168C234C4C6628B80DC1CD129024E088A67CC74020BBEA6...
  DH_GENERATOR = 2
  class DHExchange:  # Diffie-Hellman key exchange using RFC 3526 Group 14 (2048-bit).
    def __init__(self, p: int=None, g: int=None)  # Generate a new ephemeral DH key pair.
    def compute_shared_key(self, other_public: int) -> bytes  # Derive a 32-byte AES-256 key from the other side's public key.
    def format_init_message(self) -> str  # Format the CRYPTOINIT DH message for initiating key exchange.
    def format_reply_message(self) -> str  # Format the CRYPTOINIT DHREPLY message for completing key exchange.
    def parse_init_message(line: str) -> tuple  # Parse a CRYPTOINIT DH message into its components.
    def parse_reply_message(line: str) -> int  # Parse a CRYPTOINIT DHREPLY message to extract the public key.
  def encrypt(key: bytes, plaintext: bytes) -> bytes  # Encrypt plaintext using AES-256-GCM with a random IV.
  def decrypt(key: bytes, data: bytes) -> bytes  # Decrypt AES-256-GCM data produced by encrypt().
  def is_encrypted(data: bytes) -> bool  # Heuristic to detect whether a datagram contains encrypted or plaintext data.

# Persistent data storage module for the CSC shared package.
  class Data(Log):  # Extends Log.  Provides all file-backed persistence for the CSC stack.
    def __init__(self)
    def _read_json_file(self, path: Path) -> dict  # Read a JSON file from disk.  Returns {} on missing or error.
    def _write_json_file(self, path: Path, data: dict) -> bool  # Atomically write data as JSON (temp -> fsync -> rename).
    def _get_etc_dir(self) -> Path  # Return the etc/ directory via Platform (handles all OS path formats).
    def _get_run_dir(self) -> Path  # Return the runtime temp directory. Avoids instantiating Platform to prevent recursion.
    def connect(self)  # Connect to source_filename.  No-op if already connected.
    def init_data(self, source_filename='default')  # Switch the connected data file.  Resets in-memory store.
    def get_data(self, key: str, default=None)  # Read a key from the in-memory store.
    def put_data(self, key: str, value, flush=True)  # Write a key to the store and optionally flush to disk.
    def store_data(self)  # Flush the in-memory store to disk.
    def _opers_path(self) -> Path
    def _olines_conf_path(self) -> Path
    def _migrate_opers_v1_to_v2(data: dict) -> dict  # Upgrade v1 {credentials: {name: pass}} to v2 olines format.
    def _match_hostmask(mask: str, client_mask: str) -> bool  # Return True if client_mask matches the wildcard mask pattern.
    def load_opers(self) -> dict  # Public alias — backward compat for existing callers.
    def save_opers(self, data: dict) -> bool  # Public alias — backward compat for existing callers.
    def _load_opers(self) -> dict  # Load opers.json, migrating v1→v2 if needed.
    def _save_opers(self, data: dict) -> bool  # Atomically save opers.json.
    def get_olines(self) -> dict  # Return olines dict: {account: [entry, ...]}.
    def get_active_opers(self) -> list  # Return list of active oper dicts: [{nick, account, flags}].
    def get_active_opers_info(self) -> dict  # Return {nick_lower: entry_dict} for quick lookup.
    def get_oper_flags(self, nick: str) -> str  # Return flags string for an active oper nick, '' if not active.
    def protect_local_opers(self) -> bool  # Whether remote opers without O flag can KILL local opers.
    def add_active_oper(self, nick: str, account: str='', flags: str='o') -> bool  # Add or update an active oper entry.
    def remove_active_oper(self, nick: str) -> bool  # Remove an active oper entry by nick.
    def check_oper_auth(self, account: str, password: str, server_name: str, client_mask: str)  # Verify OPER credentials against o-lines.
    def parse_olines_conf(self, path=None) -> dict  # Parse olines.conf text format -> {account: [entry_dict, ...]}.

    def reload_olines(self) -> dict  # Re-parse olines.conf and update opers.json.
    def save_opers_from_server(self, server) -> bool  # Sync active_opers from server memory to opers.json.
    def run(self)
  def _get_run_dir() -> Path  # Runtime state directory — cleared each process start.
  def _get_etc_dir_static() -> Path  # Resolve etc/ without a class instance: CSC_ETC env -> walk up -> cwd/etc.

# Platform detection layer for the CSC shared package.
  class _platform:  # Thin shim replacing stdlib platform module to avoid name collision.
    def machine()
    def processor()
    def system()
    def release()
    def version()
    def python_version()
  class Platform(Version):  # Extends Version with system capability detection and persistence.
    def __init__(self)
    def configure_install_mode(self, install_at_startup=False, install_as_needed=False)  # Configure install mode after construction (called by entry points).
    def _detect_all(self)  # Run all detection routines and populate self.platform_data.
    def _detect_hardware(self)  # Detect CPU, RAM, disk, architecture.
    def _detect_cpu_speed(self)  # Detect current/max CPU speed in MHz.
    def _detect_os(self)  # Detect OS type, version, distribution.
    def _detect_virtualization(self)  # Detect if running in a VM, container, WSL, or cloud.
    def _detect_geography(self)  # Detect timezone and locale-based geography.
    def _detect_time(self)  # Check system time accuracy.
    def _detect_network(self)  # Detect hostname and IP addresses safely using passive shell commands.
    def _run_version_cmd(self, binary, args=None)  # Run a command and return version string or None.
    def _detect_software(self)  # Detect installed software tools and their versions.
    def _detect_docker(self)  # Detect Docker availability, daemon status, and resources.
    def _detect_ai_agents(self)  # Detect which AI CLI tools are available in PATH.
    def _assess_resources(self)  # Assess whether this box can handle Docker + AI workloads.
    def _detect_runtime(self)  # Detect runtime directories (temp root, agent work base) in both Windows and Linux notations.
    def agent_temp_root(self)  # Get system temp directory path.
    def agent_work_base(self)  # Get CSC agent work base directory path.
    def run_dir(self) -> Path  # Return the absolute path to the run/ directory (PROJECT_ROOT/tmp/run), creating it if needed.
    def get_etc_dir(cls) -> Path  # Return the persistent config directory (PROJECT_ROOT/etc), creating it if needed.
    def get_logs_dir(cls) -> Path  # Return the logs directory (PROJECT_ROOT/logs), creating it if needed.
    def get_tools_dir(cls) -> Path  # Return the tools/ code maps directory (irc/docs/tools), creating it if needed.

    def get_wo_dir(cls) -> Path  # Return the workorder pool directory (ops/wo).
    def get_agents_dir(cls) -> Path  # Return the agents definition/queue directory (ops/agents).
    def get_pki_dir(cls) -> Path  # Return the PKI/EasyRSA directory (etc/easy-rsa).
    def get_abs_etc_path(self, components) -> str  # Return absolute path under etc/ (platform-native separators).
    def _get_platform_json_path(self)  # Get the path for platform.json (in etc/).
    def _persist_platform(self)  # Write platform data to platform.json atomically.
    def _install_background_services(self)  # Install background service scripts (queue-worker, test-runner) when install-at-startup flag is active.
    def _setup_windows_services(self)  # Setup Windows Task Scheduler for background services.
    def _setup_unix_services(self)  # Setup Linux/macOS cron entries for background services.
    def _setup_android_services(self)  # Setup Android/Termux services (requires manual cron or SystemD user service).
    def load_platform_json(path=None)  # Load platform.json from disk. Static method for use by services.
    def get_server_shortname(cls) -> str  # Return this server's unique shortname, generating and persisting it on first call.
    def has_tool(self, tool_name)  # Check if a software tool is installed.
    def has_docker(self)  # Check if Docker is usable.
    def matches_platform(self, required_platforms)  # Check if this system matches one of the required platforms.
    def has_min_ram(self, min_ram_str)  # Check if system meets minimum RAM requirement.
    def check_requirements(self, requires=None, platform_list=None, min_ram=None)  # Check if this system satisfies all requirements.
    def check_s2s_cert(cls, config=None)  # Check the S2S TLS certificate status.
    def get_backup_dir(cls) -> Path  # Return the backup directory (PROJECT_ROOT/backup), creating it if needed.
    def refresh_platform(self)  # Re-run all detection and persist.
    def get_abs_root_path(self, components)  # Return absolute path from PROJECT_ROOT.
    def get_abs_tmp_path(self, components)  # Return absolute path from TMP directory (PROJECT_ROOT/tmp).

    def load_path(self, name)  # Retrieve a stored path reference.
    def export_env_paths(self)  # Export critical paths as environment variables.
  def _parse_size(size_str)  # Parse a human-readable size string to bytes.
  def _platform_cli(args=None)  # CLI entry point: csc-platform [subcommand]

# NickServ Service - User registration and identity management.
  class Nickserv(Service):  # NickServ service for user registration and authentication.
    def __init__(self, server_instance)  # Initialize the NickServ service.
    def _load_db(self)  # Load the nickserv database from disk into memory.
    def _save_db(self)  # Save the in-memory registry back to disk.
    def _hash_password(self, password: str) -> str  # Hash a password using MD5.
    def _verify_password(self, stored_hash: str, password: str) -> bool  # Verify a password against its stored hash.
    def register(self, email: str, password: str) -> str  # Register the current nick with an email and password.
    def _register_nick(self, nick: str, email: str, password: str) -> str  # Internal method to register a nick.
    def ident(self, password: str) -> str  # Identify with your registered nick using the password.
    def _ident_nick(self, nick: str, password: str) -> tuple  # Internal method to identify a nick.
    def unregister(self, nick: str) -> str  # Unregister a nick (oper only).
    def info(self, nick: str) -> str  # Show registration information for a nick (visible to oper/self).
    def is_registered(self, nick: str) -> bool  # Check if a nick is registered.
    def default(self, *args) -> str  # Show available NickServ commands.

  class patch(Service):  # Fuzzy patch service for applying code changes to service modules.
    def __init__(self, server_instance)
    def _find_anchor(lines, line_hint, text_fragment)  # Find a line containing text_fragment near line_hint.
    def _parse_loose_patch(content)  # Parse loose anchor-format patch content.
    def _apply_hunks(lines, hunks)  # Apply parsed hunks to a list of lines.
    def _parse_unified_diff(content)  # Parse unified diff into hunks using fuzzy anchor matching.
    def _detect_format(content)  # Detect patch format. Returns 'loose', 'unified', or None.
    def _resolve_service_path(self, service_name)  # Convert a service name to its file path.
    def apply(self, patch_filename: str) -> str  # Version target files and apply a patch from patches/ directory.
    def _apply_content(self, content)  # Apply patch content (auto-detect format).
    def revert(self, service_name: str, version: str='latest') -> str  # Revert a service file to a previous version.
    def history(self, service_name: str) -> str  # Show version/patch history for a service file.
    def default(self, *args) -> str  # Show help and patch format examples.

# Queue Worker Service: Background service for processing agent prompt queues.
  class QueueWorkerService(Service):  # Background service for processing agent prompt queues.
    def __init__(self, server_instance)
    def _load_platform(self)  # Load platform data with dual-notation paths.
    def _ensure_agent_dirs(self, agent_name)  # Auto-create agent directories from template if they don't exist.
    def _ensure_temp_dirs(self)  # Auto-create temp/runtime directories if they don't exist.
    def _get_path_for_shell(self, windows_path, use_linux=False)  # Convert path to appropriate notation for shell (bash uses Linux notation).
    def is_process_alive(self, pid)  # Check if a process with given PID is still running.

    def check_wip_complete(self, prompt_filename)  # Check if WIP file has COMPLETE as the last non-empty line.
    def add_verification_message(self, prompt_filename)  # Add verification message to incomplete workorder.
    def find_run_agent(self, agent_name)  # Find the run_agent script for an agent.
    def run_refresh_maps(self)  # Run refresh-maps to update code maps.
    def git_commit_push(self, message)  # Git add, commit, pull --rebase, and push changes.

    def process_queue_work(self)  # Check agents/*/queue/work/ for completed agents.
    def run_cycle(self)  # Run one complete queue processing cycle.
    def default(self, *args) -> str  # Show available commands.
    def cycle(self) -> str  # Run one queue processing cycle.
    def status(self) -> str  # Show queue status.

  class RunAgentExecutor:  # Execute an AI agent for a queued workorder.
    def __init__(self, agent_name: str, queue_entry_path: Path, project_root: Optional[Path]=None)  # Initialize executor with queue entry metadata.

    def _journal_complete(self) -> bool  # Write COMPLETE marker to WIP file.

    def execute(self) -> int  # Main execution flow: load, prepare, build, run, journal.

    def test_init(self)  # Test curl service initializes correctly.

    def setUp(self)  # Set up mock server instance for each test.

    def test_get_with_single_header(self, mock_get)  # Test GET request with one custom header.
    def test_get_with_multiple_headers(self, mock_get)  # Test GET request with multiple custom headers.
    def test_get_header_whitespace_trimming(self, mock_get)  # Test that header keys and values have whitespace trimmed.
    def test_get_with_200_response(self, mock_get)  # Test successful 200 response.
    def test_get_with_201_response(self, mock_get)  # Test 201 Created response.

    def test_post_with_headers(self, mock_post)  # Test POST request with body and custom headers.

    def test_post_with_empty_body(self, mock_post)  # Test POST with empty data body.
    def test_post_with_special_characters(self, mock_post)  # Test POST with special characters in body.
    def test_post_notification_service(self, mock_post)  # Test real-world use case: ntfy.sh notification.

    def test_connection_timeout(self, mock_get)  # Test connection timeout error handling.
    def test_invalid_url_from_requests(self, mock_get)  # Test invalid URL error from requests library.
    def test_connection_error_dns(self, mock_get)  # Test DNS resolution failure.
    def test_request_exception(self, mock_post)  # Test generic request exception handling.

    def test_header_with_multiple_colons(self, mock_get)  # Test header value containing colons (e.g., URL).

  class TestDebounce:
    def test_debounce_batches_rapid_changes(self, tmp_path)  # Multiple rapid on_change calls result in a single _flush call.
    def test_debounce_resets_on_new_change(self, tmp_path)  # A new change arriving during the debounce window resets the timer.

    def test_hash_changes_on_remove(self, tmp_path)
    def test_hash_changes_on_modify(self, tmp_path)

    def test_hash_is_hex_string(self, tmp_path)

    def test_dele_called_for_deleted_file(self, mock_connect, tmp_path)  # _flush sends DELE for deleted files.
    def test_filelist_hash_uploaded_after_push(self, mock_connect, tmp_path)  # _flush uploads wo/.filelist.hash after pushing file changes.
    def test_ftp_connect_params(self, mock_connect, tmp_path)  # WoWatcher connects with correct host and port.
  class TestSlaveDeltaSync:

    def test_no_sync_when_hashes_match(self, tmp_path)  # If local and remote hashes match, no files are downloaded.
    def test_downloads_missing_file(self, tmp_path)  # If remote has a file not present locally, it is downloaded.
    def test_skips_unchanged_files(self, tmp_path)  # Files whose local size matches remote size are not re-downloaded.
  def _make_watcher(tmp_path, **kwargs)  # Return a WoWatcher pointing at tmp_path with FTP disabled by default.

# Batch API Tool Loop Executor
  ESCALATION_CHAIN = ['claude-haiku-4-5-20251001', 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514', 'claude-opus-4-2025...
  DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES = 2
  LOGS_DIR = CSC_ROOT / 'logs'
  TOOLS = [{'name': 'read_file', 'description': 'Read the contents of a file', 'input_s...
  class BatchToolExecutor:  # Execute batch tool loops until completion with retry and escalation support.
    def __init__(self, api_key: str, max_retries: int=DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES)
    def verbose_log(self, msg: str, level: str='INFO')  # Log with CSC logging if available, store in execution log.
    def get_current_model(self) -> str  # Get the current model from escalation chain.
    def reset_for_phase(self)  # Reset retry/escalation state for a new phase.
    def should_escalate(self) -> bool  # Check if we should escalate to next model.
    def escalate_model(self) -> str  # Escalate to next model in chain. Returns new model name.
    def _normalize_path(self, path: str) -> str  # Convert Cygwin paths (/c/...) to Windows paths (C:\...) for Python file ops.
    def _try_alternative_paths(self, path: str) -> list  # Generate alternative paths to try if primary path fails.
    def execute_tool(self, name: str, input_data: dict) -> tuple[str, bool, float]  # Execute a tool and return (result_text, is_error, execution_time_ms).
    def wait_for_batch(self, batch_id: str, max_wait: int=3600) -> dict  # Poll batch status with exponential backoff until complete or timeout.
    def process_batch(self, batch_id: str, phase_name: str='') -> dict  # Process a single batch: retrieve results, execute tools, return state.
    def submit_followup_batch(self, custom_id: str, model: str, system_prompt: str, messages: list[dict], tool_results: list[dict]) -> str  # Submit a follow-up batch with tool results.
    def execute_batch_loop(self, initial_batch_id: str, phase_name: str='', max_iterations: int=20) -> dict  # Execute the full tool loop for a batch until end_turn.
    def verify_phase_success(self, result: dict) -> tuple[bool, str]  # Verify that a phase completed successfully.
    def execute_phase_with_retry(self, batch_id: str, phase_name: str, max_iterations: int=20, system_prompt: Optional[str]=None) -> dict  # Execute a phase with retry logic and model escalation.
  def get_api_key() -> str  # Extract API key from .env file or environment.
  def log(msg: str, level: str='INFO')  # Log message with timestamp.
  def get_model_short_name(model: str) -> str  # Get short display name for model.
  def main()

# CSC Bridge - IRC protocol bridge proxy for CSC ecosystem

── tmp/csc/clones/opus/upgrade_server-1773366886/repo/packages/csc-service/csc_service/bridge/bridge.py ──
# Core bridge orchestrator -- bridges inbound clients to the CSC server.
  class Bridge:  # Bridges multiple inbound transports to an outbound transport.
    def __init__(self, inbound_transports: List[InboundTransport], outbound_transport: Optional[OutboundTransport], session_timeout: int=300, encrypt: bool=False, normalize_mode: Optional[str]=None, daemon_mode: bool=False)  # Initialize the bridge with transports and configuration.
    def start(self)  # Start all transports and background threads.
    def stop(self)  # Stop all transports, destroy all sessions, and clean up.
    def _on_client_data(self, data: bytes, client_id: ClientID, inbound: InboundTransport)  # Callback invoked by inbound transports when data arrives from a client.
    def _create_session(self, client_id: ClientID, inbound: InboundTransport) -> Optional[ClientSession]  # Create a new session for a client.
    def _handle_disconnect(self, client_id: ClientID)  # Handle a client disconnect event.
    def _destroy_session(self, session: ClientSession)  # Clean up and destroy a session, closing server connection gracefully.
    def _sniff_nick(self, session: ClientSession, data: bytes)  # Extract and store the nick from NICK commands in client data.
    def _forward_to_server(self, session: ClientSession, data: bytes)  # Forward client data to the CSC server with encryption and normalization.
    def _handle_control_command(self, session: ClientSession, data: bytes)  # Delegate control command handling to the session's control handler.
    def _forward_to_client(self, session: ClientSession, data: bytes)  # Forward server data back to the client with optional normalization.
    def _server_listener(self, session: ClientSession)  # Poll server for responses and forward to client (per-session thread).
    def _keepalive_loop(self)  # Send periodic keepalive PING to idle sessions (background thread).
    def _cleanup_loop(self)  # Remove sessions that have been idle longer than session_timeout.
    def session_count(self) -> int  # Return the number of active sessions.
    def list_sessions(self) -> List[dict]  # Return a list of summary dicts for all active sessions.
    def get_lobby_sessions(self) -> List[ClientSession]  # Return a list of all sessions currently in the LOBBY state.

  class ControlHandler:  # Handles IRC protocol logic for clients in the LOBBY state.
    def __init__(self, session, bridge)  # Initializes the control handler, storing references to session and bridge, and setting up authentication state.
    def handle_line(self, line: bytes)  # Processes a raw line from the client, handling authentication, keepalive, and control commands.
    def _handle_command(self, text)  # Parses and executes /trans commands including connect, history, fav (favorite), and menu.
    def _subcmd_connect(self, args)  # Handler for 'connect' subcommand.

    def _subcmd_menu(self, args)  # Handler for 'menu' subcommand.

    def _try_auth(self)  # Attempts authentication once both nick and username are provided, validating against BridgeData.

    def _send_numeric(self, numeric, text)  # Send a numeric IRC reply to the current client.
    def send_notice(self, text)  # Send an IRC NOTICE message to the current client.
    def _send_raw(self, line)  # Send a raw IRC line to the client over the inbound transport.
    def _broadcast_lobby_message(self, message: str)  # Broadcast a chat message to all other clients in the lobby.

# Data persistence for the Bridge Daemon.
  class BridgeData(Data):  # - What it does: Manages persistent storage for the Bridge Daemon, including
    def __init__(self)  # Initializes BridgeData by loading bridge_data.json and ensuring the "users" key exists.
    def create_user(self, username, password)  # Creates a new user account with salted password hash, empty history, favorites, and settings.
    def validate_user(self, username, password)  # Validates user credentials by comparing the provided password's salted hash against the stored hash.
    def add_history(self, username, conn_str)  # Adds a connection string to the user's history list, moving it to the top if already present.
    def get_history(self, username) -> List[str]  # Retrieve the connection history for a user.
    def set_favorite(self, username, alias, conn_str)  # Stores a connection string under an alias in the user's favorites dictionary.
    def get_favorite(self, username, alias) -> Optional[str]  # Retrieves a connection string from the user's favorites by alias.
    def get_favorites(self, username) -> Dict[str, str]  # Retrieves all favorites for a user as a dictionary mapping alias to connection string.
    def _hash_password(self, password, salt)  # Generates a SHA256 hash of the password concatenated with the salt.

  class IRCMessage:  # Represents a parsed IRC message conforming to RFC 1459/2812 wire format.

── tmp/csc/clones/opus/upgrade_server-1773366886/repo/packages/csc-service/csc_service/bridge/irc_normalizer.py ──
# Protocol normalization layer for the CSC bridge.
  class IrcNormalizer:  # Handles stateful normalization of IRC messages between dialects.
    def __init__(self, mode: str)  # Initializes the normalizer with the specified mode and sets up state tracking for registration progress.
    def normalize_client_to_server(self, block: str, session) -> Optional[str]  # Normalizes a block of text from client to server, splitting on CR-LF and processing each line.
    def _normalize_client_line(self, line: str, session) -> Optional[str]  # Normalizes a single IRC line from the client based on the configured mode.
    def normalize_server_to_client(self, block: str, session) -> Optional[str]  # Normalizes a block of text from server to client, splitting on CR-LF and processing each line.
    def _normalize_server_line(self, line: str, session) -> Optional[str]  # Normalizes a single IRC line from the server based on the configured mode.
    def _handle_cap_ls(self, session, msg)  # Handler for CAP LS and CAP LIST subcommands.
    def _handle_cap_req(self, session, msg)  # Handler for CAP REQ subcommand.
    def _handle_cap_end(self, session, msg)  # Handler for CAP END subcommand.
    def _send_notice(self, session, text: str)  # Sends a local NOTICE message to the client using the session's inbound transport.
    def _send_numeric(self, session, numeric: str, target: str, text: str)  # Sends a local numeric reply to the client using the session's inbound transport.
    def _send_raw_to_client(self, session, raw_line: str)  # Send raw data back to the client using session.inbound.

── tmp/csc/clones/opus/upgrade_server-1773366886/repo/packages/csc-service/csc_service/bridge/transports/__init__.py ──

── tmp/csc/clones/opus/upgrade_server-1773366886/repo/packages/csc-service/csc_service/bridge/transports/base.py ──
# Abstract base classes for inbound and outbound transports.
  class InboundTransport(ABC):  # Accepts connections/datagrams from clients and delivers data to the bridge.
    def start(self, on_data: Callable[[bytes, ClientID], None]) -> None  # Start listening for client connections/datagrams.
    def send_to_client(self, client_id: ClientID, data: bytes) -> None  # Send data back to a specific connected client.
    def stop(self) -> None  # Stop listening, close all client connections, and release resources.
    def remove_client(self, client_id: ClientID) -> None  # Clean up resources for a specific disconnected client.
  class OutboundTransport(ABC):  # Sends data upstream to the CSC server on behalf of proxied clients.
    def create_upstream(self, session_id: str) -> UpstreamHandle  # Create a new upstream connection/socket for a client session.
    def send(self, handle: UpstreamHandle, data: bytes) -> None  # Send data to the CSC server through a session's upstream handle.
    def recv(self, handle: UpstreamHandle, timeout: float=1.0) -> Optional[bytes]  # Receive data from the CSC server on a session's upstream handle.
    def close(self, handle: UpstreamHandle) -> None  # Close an upstream handle and release its resources.

── tmp/csc/clones/opus/upgrade_server-1773366886/repo/packages/csc-service/csc_service/bridge/transports/tcp_inbound.py ──
# TCP inbound transport -- listens for standard IRC clients over TCP.
  class TCPClientID:  # Identifier for a TCP client connection.
    def __init__(self, conn: socket.socket, addr: tuple, conn_id: int)  # Initialize a TCP client identifier.
    def __hash__(self)  # Hash based on connection ID for use as dictionary key.
    def __eq__(self, other)  # Compare equality based on connection ID.
    def __repr__(self)  # Return a human-readable representation for logging.
  class TCPInbound(InboundTransport):  # Accepts TCP connections from IRC clients, line-buffers on \r\n.
    def __init__(self, host: str='127.0.0.1', port: int=6667)  # Initialize the TCP inbound transport.
    def start(self, on_data: Callable[[bytes, ClientID], None]) -> None  # Bind the server socket, start listening, and launch the accept thread.
    def _accept_loop(self)  # Accept loop running on the accept thread.
    def _handle_client(self, client_id: TCPClientID)  # Read IRC lines from a TCP client, delivering each complete line.
    def send_to_client(self, client_id: ClientID, data: bytes) -> None  # Send data to a specific connected TCP client.
    def remove_client(self, client_id: ClientID) -> None  # Remove a client connection and close its socket.
    def _cleanup_client(self, client_id: TCPClientID)  # Remove a client from tracking and close its socket.
    def stop(self) -> None  # Stop accepting connections, close all clients, and shut down.

── tmp/csc/clones/opus/upgrade_server-1773366886/repo/packages/csc-service/csc_service/bridge/transports/tcp_outbound.py ──
# TCP outbound transport -- sends data upstream to the CSC server via TCP.
  class TCPUpstreamHandle:  # Wraps a single TCP connection dedicated to one client session.
    def __init__(self, sock: socket.socket, server_addr: tuple)  # Initialize the upstream handle with a connected socket.
    def close(self)  # Close the underlying TCP connection.
  class TCPOutbound(OutboundTransport):  # Creates per-session TCP connections for forwarding traffic to the CSC server.
    def __init__(self, server_host: str='127.0.0.1', server_port: int=9525)  # Initialize the TCP outbound transport with the target server address.
    def create_upstream(self, session_id: str) -> TCPUpstreamHandle  # Establish a new TCP connection to the server for a client session.
    def send(self, handle: TCPUpstreamHandle, data: bytes) -> None  # Send data to the CSC server through a session's TCP connection.
    def recv(self, handle: TCPUpstreamHandle, timeout: float=1.0) -> Optional[bytes]  # Receive data from the CSC server on a session's TCP connection.
    def close(self, handle: TCPUpstreamHandle) -> None  # Close an upstream TCP connection and remove it from tracking.

── tmp/csc/clones/opus/upgrade_server-1773366886/repo/packages/csc-service/csc_service/bridge/transports/udp_inbound.py ──
# UDP inbound transport -- listens for CSC client datagrams.
  class UDPInbound(InboundTransport):  # Accepts UDP datagrams from CSC clients on a single bound socket.
    def __init__(self, host: str='127.0.0.1', port: int=9526)  # Initialize the UDP inbound transport.
    def start(self, on_data: Callable[[bytes, ClientID], None]) -> None  # Bind the socket and start the background listener thread.
    def _listen_loop(self)  # Main receive loop running on the listener thread.
    def send_to_client(self, client_id: ClientID, data: bytes) -> None  # Send a datagram back to a specific CSC client.
    def remove_client(self, client_id: ClientID) -> None  # Remove a client from internal tracking.
    def stop(self) -> None  # Stop the listener thread and close the UDP socket.

  class Client(Network):  # UDP client with IRC protocol support, identity, macros, aliases, and text-file uploads.
    def __init__(self, config_path=None, input_file=None, output_file=None)  # Initializes the instance.
    def _load_config(self)  # Loads client configuration from JSON, creating defaults as needed.
    def _save_config(self)  # Saves all client data back to config file.
    def _disconnect_gracefully(self)  # Send QUIT and close connection.
    def _connect_to_server(self, host, port)  # Establish new server connection and re-register.
    def _configure_translator(self, host, port)  # Configure translator proxy and reconnect.
    def _disable_translator(self)  # Disable translator and connect directly to server.
    def _add_to_server_history(self, host, port)  # Add server to history (max 3 entries).
    def _handle_server_command(self, args: str)  # /server <host> [port]
    def _handle_reconnect_command(self)  # /reconnect
    def _handle_disconnect_command(self)  # /disconnect
    def _handle_translator_command(self, args: str)  # /translator <host> <port>  - Configure translator proxy
    def _handle_status_command(self)  # /status
    def _handle_ping_command(self)  # /ping
    def identify(self)  # Send IRC registration sequence: NICK + USER.
    def _write_to_output(self, text)  # Write text to output_file if set, always also print to stdout.
    def run(self, interactive=True)  # Starts listener thread and handles input.
    def _is_fifo(self)  # Return True if input_file is a POSIX FIFO.
    def _input_loop_file(self)  # Input loop for --infile / --fifo mode.
    def _input_loop(self)  # Handles blocking user input in a separate thread.
    def _handle_server_message_data(self, msg_data)  # Decodes and routes a message received from the server.
    def _handle_irc_line(self, line)  # Parse and handle a single IRC line from the server.
    def _handle_numeric(self, parsed)  # Handle IRC numeric replies.
    def _is_authorized(self, nick, channel=None)  # Check if a nick is authorized to command this client.
    def _handle_privmsg_recv(self, parsed)  # Handle received PRIVMSG, including nick-prefixed command execution.
    def _handle_local_file_session_line(self, sender_nick, text, target)  # Handle a line during an active local file upload session.
    def _handle_notice_recv(self, parsed)  # Handle received NOTICE, including ISOP responses.
    def handle_server_message(self, msg: str)  # Legacy handler — prints messages received from the server.
    def process_command(self, cmd: str)  # Processes user commands with alias/macro expansion.
    def print_local_help(self)  # Displays available client-side commands.
    def send_file(self, filepath: str)  # Sends a text file to the server as PRIVMSG to current channel.

  class JulesMonitor(Data):  # Monitor Jules sessions, validate plans, send feedback autonomously.
    def __init__(self, csc_root: str=None)  # Initialize JulesMonitor with paths and API config.
    def _initialize_paths(self, csc_root: str=None) -> None  # Resolve CSC root directory.

    def _handle_completion(self, session: dict) -> None  # Process a completed session — log results, extract PR URL.
    def _handle_failure(self, session: dict) -> None  # Process a failed session — log error.
    def run_cycle(self) -> None  # Poll Jules sessions and handle each based on state.
  def run_cycle(csc_root: str=None) -> None  # Entry point called by service main loop.

# wo_sync_client.py — Slave-side sync client for ops/wo/.
  class WoSyncClient:  # Slave-side sync client: compares filelist hash, downloads deltas.
    def __init__(self, wo_dir: str, ftp_master_host: str, ftp_master_port: int=9521, ftp_user: str='csc-node', ftp_password: str='', poll_interval_s: int=30)
    def sync(self) -> bool  # Perform one sync cycle.  Returns True if files were updated.
    def run(self) -> None  # Run continuously, syncing every poll_interval_s.  Blocks.
    def stop(self) -> None  # Signal run() to exit on next iteration.
    def _connect(self) -> ftplib.FTP_TLS
    def _fetch_remote_hash(self, ftp: ftplib.FTP_TLS) -> Optional[str]  # Download wo/.filelist.hash from master.  Returns hash string or None.
    def _fetch_remote_listing(self, ftp: ftplib.FTP_TLS) -> Dict[str, Tuple[int, str]]  # Return {rel_path: (size, mtime_str)} from master's wo/ directory.
    def _parse_list_line(self, line: str, prefix: str, listing: Dict[str, Tuple[int, str]]) -> None  # Parse one LIST line and recurse into subdirectories (not implemented here
    def _fetch_mlsd_listing(self, ftp: ftplib.FTP_TLS, remote_dir: str='wo') -> Dict[str, int]  # Return {rel_path: size} using MLSD (RFC 3659), recursing into subdirs.
    def _download_file(self, ftp: ftplib.FTP_TLS, rel_path: str) -> None  # Download wo/<rel_path> from master into local wo_dir.
    def _local_sizes(self) -> Dict[str, int]  # Return {rel_path: size} for all local files under wo_dir.
    def _do_sync(self, ftp: ftplib.FTP_TLS) -> bool  # Core sync logic.  Returns True if any files were downloaded.

── tmp/csc/clones/opus/upgrade_server-1773366886/repo/packages/csc-service/csc_service/infra/wo_watcher.py ──
# wo_watcher.py — Filesystem watcher for ops/wo/.
  class WoWatcher:  # Monitors ops/wo/ and pushes changes through the FTP master.
    def __init__(self, wo_dir: str, ftp_master_host: str, ftp_master_port: int=9521, ftp_user: str='csc-node', ftp_password: str='', debounce_ms: int=500, poll_interval_s: int=5, on_change_hook: Optional[Callable[[str], None]]=None)
    def start(self) -> None  # Start watching. Blocks until stop() is called.
    def stop(self) -> None  # Signal the watcher to stop.
    def on_change(self, changed_path: str) -> None  # Called when any file in ops/wo/ changes.  Schedules debounced push.
    def on_delete(self, deleted_path: str) -> None  # Called when a file is deleted from ops/wo/.
    def _reschedule_debounce(self) -> None  # (Re-)schedule the debounce timer.  Must be called with self._lock held.
    def _flush(self) -> None  # Push accumulated changes to the FTP master.
    def _start_inotify(self) -> None  # Use inotify_simple if available, else fall back to poll.
    def _run_inotify_simple(self, inotify_simple) -> None  # Block on inotify events until stop() is called.
    def _start_watchdog_or_poll(self) -> None  # Use watchdog if available, otherwise fall back to polling.
    def _build_snapshot(self) -> Dict[str, tuple]  # Return {rel_path: (size, mtime)} for all files under wo_dir.
    def _start_poll_loop(self) -> None  # Block, polling every poll_interval_s until stop() is called.
  def compute_filelist_hash(wo_dir: Path) -> str  # Return SHA-256 of sorted 'relpath:size:mtime' entries under wo_dir.
  def _ftp_push_file(ftp: ftplib.FTP_TLS, local_path: Path, remote_path: str) -> None  # Upload local_path to remote_path on the FTP connection.
  def _ftp_delete_file(ftp: ftplib.FTP_TLS, remote_path: str) -> None  # Delete remote_path on the FTP connection.
  def _connect_ftp(host: str, port: int, user: str, password: str) -> ftplib.FTP_TLS  # Open an authenticated FTP_TLS connection.

# PKI enrollment WSGI server.
  _SHORTNAME_RE = re.compile('^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9.\\-]{0,62}[a-z0-9]$')
  EASYRSA_DIR = Platform.PROJECT_ROOT / 'etc' / 'easy-rsa'
  EASYRSA_BIN = EASYRSA_DIR / ('easyrsa.bat' if os.name == 'nt' else 'easyrsa')
  PKI_DIR = EASYRSA_DIR / 'pki'
  CA_CRT = PKI_DIR / 'ca.crt'
  CRL_PEM = PKI_DIR / 'crl.pem'
  ISSUED_DIR = PKI_DIR / 'issued'
  TOKEN_FILE = plat.run_dir / 'pki_tokens.json'
  APPROVED_FILE = Path('/opt/csc/irc/etc/approved_servers.json')
  PKI_LOG = Platform.get_logs_dir() / 'pki.log'
  CERT_DIR = Platform.get_etc_dir()
  TOKEN_TTL = 86400
  BIND_HOST = '127.0.0.1'
  BIND_PORT = 9530
  class PKIRequestHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):  # HTTP request handler for PKI enrollment endpoints.
    def log_message(self, format, *args)  # Suppress default HTTP logging — we use PKI log instead.
    def _send_json(self, code, data)  # Send a JSON response.
    def _send_file(self, path, content_type='application/x-pem-file')  # Send a file as response.
    def _read_body(self)  # Read and parse JSON request body.
    def do_GET(self)  # Handle GET requests.
    def do_POST(self)  # Handle POST requests.
    def _handle_enroll(self)  # Handle certificate enrollment request.
    def _handle_renew(self)  # Handle certificate renewal via mTLS.
  def _load_approved()  # Load approved server list from repo-tracked JSON. Returns dict shortname → metadata.
  def _load_tokens()  # Load token store from disk.
  def _save_tokens(tokens)  # Atomically save token store.
  def _write_pki_log(message)  # Append a timestamped entry to the PKI log.

── tmp/csc/clones/opus/upgrade_server-1773366886/repo/packages/csc-service/csc_service/server/channel.py ──
# Channel and ChannelManager for IRC-style channel support.
  class Channel:  # Represents a single IRC channel.
    def __init__(self, name: str)  # Initializes the instance.
    def add_member(self, nick: str, addr: tuple, modes: Optional[Set[str]]=None)  # Add a member to the channel.
    def remove_member(self, nick: str)  # Remove a member from the channel.
    def has_member(self, nick: str) -> bool  # Check if nick is in this channel.
    def get_names_list(self) -> str  # Return space-separated names list with @/+ prefix for ops/voiced.
    def member_count(self) -> int  # Returns the number of members in the channel.
    def is_op(self, nick: str) -> bool  # Check if nick has channel operator mode.
    def has_voice(self, nick: str) -> bool  # Check if nick has voice (+v) mode.
    def can_speak(self, nick: str) -> bool  # Check if nick can send messages to this channel.
    def can_set_topic(self, nick: str) -> bool  # Check if nick can set the channel topic.
  class ChannelManager:  # Manages all channels on the server.
    def __init__(self)  # Initializes the instance.
    def ensure_channel(self, name: str) -> Channel  # Create channel if it doesn't exist, return it.
    def get_channel(self, name: str) -> Optional[Channel]  # Get channel by name, or None.
    def remove_channel(self, name: str) -> bool  # Remove a channel. Cannot remove the default channel.
    def list_channels(self) -> List[Channel]  # Return all channels.
    def find_channels_for_nick(self, nick: str) -> List[Channel]  # Find all channels a nick is a member of.
    def remove_nick_from_all(self, nick: str) -> List[str]  # Remove a nick from all channels. Returns list of channel names they were in.

# Diffie-Hellman key exchange and AES-256-GCM encryption for the CSC translator.
  DH_PRIME = int('FFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFC90FDAA22168C234C4C6628B80DC1CD129024E088A67CC74020BBEA6...
  DH_GENERATOR = 2
  class DHExchange:  # Diffie-Hellman key exchange using RFC 3526 Group 14 (2048-bit).
    def __init__(self)  # Generate a new ephemeral DH key pair.
    def compute_shared_key(self, other_public: int) -> bytes  # Derive a 32-byte AES-256 key from the other side's public key.
    def format_init_message(self) -> str  # Format the CRYPTOINIT DH message for initiating key exchange.
    def format_reply_message(self) -> str  # Format the CRYPTOINIT DHREPLY message for completing key exchange.
    def parse_init_message(line: str) -> tuple  # Parse a CRYPTOINIT DH message into its components.
    def parse_reply_message(line: str) -> int  # Parse a CRYPTOINIT DHREPLY message to extract the public key.
  def encrypt(key: bytes, plaintext: bytes) -> bytes  # Encrypt plaintext using AES-256-GCM with a random IV.
  def decrypt(key: bytes, data: bytes) -> bytes  # Decrypt AES-256-GCM data produced by encrypt().
  def is_encrypted(data: bytes) -> bool  # Heuristic to detect whether a datagram contains encrypted or plaintext data.

    def __init__(self, host='0.0.0.0', port=9525, timeout=120)  # Initializes the Server.
    def client_registry(self)  # Dynamic property that reads client registry from disk.

    def opers(self)  # Set of lowercase nicks currently holding any oper status.

    def oper_has_flag(self, nick, flag)  # Public alias for _oper_has_flag (used by message handler).

    def is_local_oper(self, nick)  # Local oper: any oper flag (o, O, a, A).
    def is_global_oper(self, nick)  # Global oper: O flag.
    def is_server_admin(self, nick)  # Server admin: a or A flag.
    def is_net_admin(self, nick)  # Network admin: A flag.
    def wakewords(self)  # Dynamic property that reads wakewords from disk on every access.
    def sync_from_disk(self)  # Reload state from disk ONLY if files have changed.
    def _cleanup_loop(self)  # Periodic background loop to remove timed-out clients.
    def _botserv_log_monitor_loop(self)  # Background loop for BotServ log monitoring and echoing.

    def _run_cleanup_once(self)  # Runs the client cleanup logic once.
    def _thread_worker(self, data, addr)  # Worker thread to process each incoming packet.
    def sock_send(self, data, addr)  # Override sock_send to encrypt data if a key is established for addr.
    def _network_loop(self)  # Background loop for processing all incoming network messages.
    def broadcast(self, message, exclude=None)  # Sends a message to all active clients.
    def broadcast_to_channel(self, channel_name, message, exclude=None)  # Send a message to all members of a channel.
    def send_to_nick(self, nick, message)  # Send a message to a specific nick by looking up their address.
    def send_wallops(self, message)  # Send a WALLOPS message to all connected IRC operators.
    def old_broadcast(self, message, exclude=None)  # Sends a message to all active client addresses.
    def sync_persistent_clients(self)  # Writes the in-memory client registry back to persistent storage.
    def _persist_session_data(self)  # Persist current session data to separate JSON files atomically.
    def run(self)  # Starts the server's main network loop and the terminal interface.
    def _wait_for_shutdown(self)  # Block the main thread until SIGTERM/SIGINT is received or SHUTDOWN file exists.
    def get_data(self, key)  # Wrapper for persistent data retrieval.
    def put_data(self, key, value)  # Wrapper for persistent data writes.

# IRC-compliant message handler for csc-server.
  NICK_RE = re.compile('^[A-Za-z\\[\\]\\\\`_^{|}][A-Za-z0-9\\[\\]\\\\`_^{|}\\-]*$')
  class MessageHandler:  # Handles all incoming UDP messages from clients, acting as the central router
    def __init__(self, server, file_handler)  # Initializes the instance.
    def process(self, data, addr)  # Process a raw UDP datagram from a client.
    def _handle_file_session_line(self, addr, line_stripped, raw_line)  # Handle a line while in an active file upload session.
    def _dispatch_irc_command(self, msg, addr, raw_line)  # Route an IRC command to the appropriate handler.
    def _ensure_reg_state(self, addr)  # Ensure registration state exists for addr.
    def _handle_pass(self, msg, addr)  # PASS <password>
    def _handle_nick(self, msg, addr)  # NICK <nickname>
    def _handle_user(self, msg, addr)  # USER <username> <mode> <unused> :<realname>
    def _try_complete_registration(self, addr)  # Check if both NICK and USER have been received; if so, complete registration.
    def _handle_join(self, msg, addr)  # JOIN <channel>[,<channel>...]
    def _handle_part(self, msg, addr)  # PART <channel>[,<channel>...] [:<reason>]

    def _broadcast_privmsg_filtered(self, channel, out_msg, message_text, sender_nick, exclude=None)  # Broadcast a PRIVMSG to channel members with wakeword filtering.
    def _handle_service_via_chatline(self, raw_line, addr, nick, channel=None)  # Handle service commands received via chatline (e.g., AI 1 agent assign...).
    def _send_notice(self, addr, text)  # Helper to send a NOTICE message to a client.

    def _handle_away(self, msg, addr)  # AWAY [:<message>]
    def _handle_mode(self, msg, addr)  # MODE <target> <modestring> [param1] [param2] ...

    def _handle_kick(self, msg, addr)  # KICK <channel> <nick> [:<reason>]
    def _handle_kill(self, msg, addr)  # KILL <nick> [:<reason>] — requires oper 'kill' flag.

    def _trust_addhost(self, addr, args)
    def _trust_delhost(self, addr, args)
    def _write_olines_conf(self, data)  # Rewrite olines.conf from current opers.json data.
    def _handle_stats(self, msg, addr)  # STATS <letter> — server statistics (oper only).
    def _handle_rehash(self, msg, addr)  # REHASH — reload olines.conf into memory (requires server admin).
    def _handle_shutdown(self, msg, addr)  # SHUTDOWN [reason] — graceful server shutdown (requires server admin).
    def _handle_link(self, msg, addr)  # LINK <server> [port] — initiate S2S link.
    def _handle_relink(self, msg, addr)  # RELINK <server> — reconnect a dropped S2S link.
    def _handle_delink(self, msg, addr)  # DELINK <server> [reason] — drop S2S link (alias for SQUIT).
    def _handle_help(self, msg, addr)  # HELP — show available commands based on caller's oper flags.
    def _handle_localconfig(self, msg, addr)  # LOCALCONFIG <show|list|get|set|del> [key] [value]

── tmp/csc/clones/opus/upgrade_server-1773366886/repo/packages/csc-service/csc_service/server/server_s2s.py ──
# Server-to-Server (S2S) linking protocol for CSC IRC server federation.
  S2S_CRLF = b'\r\n'
  S2S_MAX_LINE = 8192
  class ServerLink:  # Represents a single UDP connection to a peer CSC server with DH encryption.
    def __init__(self, local_server, remote_host, remote_port, password, remote_server_id=None, sock=None)  # Initialize a server link.
    def connect(self)  # Establish UDP connection to remote server and initiate DH key exchange.
    def _initiate_dh_exchange(self)  # Send CRYPTOINIT DH message to initiate key exchange.
    def _handle_dh_reply(self, pubkey_hex)  # Process CRYPTOINIT DHREPLY message and derive AES key.
    def _send_dh_reply(self, pubkey_hex)  # Process incoming CRYPTOINIT DH and send DHREPLY with AES derivation.
    def authenticate(self)  # Exchange SLINK/SLINKACK handshake after DH key exchange.
    def handle_inbound_handshake(self)  # Handle authentication for an inbound (accepted) connection.
    def start_reader(self, callback)  # Start a background thread that reads S2S messages and calls callback.
    def _reader_loop(self, callback)  # Background loop reading UDP datagrams and processing S2S messages.
    def send_message(self, command, *args)  # Send an S2S command to the remote server (encrypted if key available).
    def send_raw(self, line)  # Send a raw line to the remote server (encrypted if key available).
    def is_connected(self)  # Check if this link is alive and authenticated.
    def close(self)  # Gracefully close the link.
    def _recv_line(self, timeout=None)  # Read one CRLF-terminated line from the socket.
    def _get_local_server_id(self)  # Return the local server's unique ID.
    def _log(self, message)  # Log via the local server's logger.
  class ServerNetwork:  # Manages all linked servers and network-wide operations.
    def __init__(self, local_server)  # Initialize the server network manager.
    def start_listener(self)  # Start the UDP listener for inbound S2S connections with DH encryption.
    def _receive_loop(self)  # Receive inbound S2S UDP datagrams from peers with DH and SLINK auth.
    def _handle_inbound_link(self, link)  # Handle a newly established inbound UDP S2S link.
    def link_to(self, host, port, password=None)  # Initiate an outbound link to a remote server.
    def get_peer_servers(self)  # List all connected peer server IDs.
    def get_link(self, server_id)  # Get the ServerLink for a specific peer.
    def broadcast_to_network(self, command, args_str='', exclude_server=None)  # Send an S2S command to all connected peers.
    def get_user_from_network(self, nick)  # Find a user on any server in the network.
    def get_channel_from_network(self, channel)  # Find a channel on any server in the network.
    def route_message(self, source_nick, target, text, exclude_server=None)  # Route a PRIVMSG/NOTICE across the network.
    def sync_line(self, target_nick, line, exclude_server=None)  # Route a raw IRC line to a specific user on the network.
    def sync_user_join(self, nick, host, modes, channel=None, exclude_server=None)  # Notify the network that a user joined/connected.
    def sync_user_quit(self, nick, reason='', exclude_server=None)  # Notify the network that a user disconnected.
    def sync_user_part(self, nick, channel, reason='', exclude_server=None)  # Notify the network that a user parted a channel.
    def sync_nick_change(self, old_nick, new_nick, exclude_server=None)  # Notify the network that a user changed their nickname.
    def sync_channel(self, channel_name, modes_str, members_json, exclude_server=None)  # Broadcast channel state to the network.
    def sync_topic(self, channel_name, topic, exclude_server=None)  # Broadcast channel topic to the network.
    def sync_channel_state(self, chan_name, exclude_server=None)  # Helper to sync current state of a local channel to the network.
    def _send_full_sync(self, link)  # Send complete local state to a newly linked peer.
    def _dispatch_s2s_message(self, link, command, rest)  # Route an incoming S2S command to the appropriate handler.
    def _handle_syncuser(self, link, rest)  # Handle SYNCUSER: add/update a remote user in tracking.
    def _handle_synpart(self, link, rest)  # Handle SYNPART: remote user leaves a channel.
    def _handle_syncnick(self, link, rest)  # Handle SYNCNICK: remote user changes nickname.
    def _handle_syncchan(self, link, rest)  # Handle SYNCCHAN: merge remote channel state.
    def _handle_synctopic(self, link, rest)  # Handle SYNCTOPIC: remote channel topic update.
    def _handle_syncmsg(self, link, rest)  # Handle SYNCMSG: deliver a message from the network locally.
    def _handle_syncline(self, link, rest)  # Handle SYNCLINE: deliver a raw IRC line to a local user.
    def _handle_desync(self, link, rest)  # Handle DESYNC: remove a nick or channel from remote tracking.
    def _handle_squit(self, link, rest)  # Handle SQUIT: a server is disconnecting.
    def _handle_error(self, link, rest)  # Handle ERROR message from a peer.
    def _handle_link_lost(self, link)  # Called when a link's reader thread detects a disconnect.
    def _remove_server_state(self, server_id)  # Remove all remote state belonging to a disconnected server.
    def _rename_local_user(self, old_nick, new_nick)  # Rename a local user due to nick collision.
    def shutdown(self)  # Shut down all S2S connections and the listener.
    def _get_local_server_id(self)  # Return the local server's unique ID.
    def _log(self, message)  # Log via the local server's logger.

# Diffie-Hellman key exchange and AES-256-GCM encryption for the CSC translator.
  DH_PRIME = int('FFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFC90FDAA22168C234C4C6628B80DC1CD129024E088A67CC74020BBEA6...
  DH_GENERATOR = 2
  class DHExchange:  # Diffie-Hellman key exchange using RFC 3526 Group 14 (2048-bit).
    def __init__(self, p: int=None, g: int=None)  # Generate a new ephemeral DH key pair.
    def compute_shared_key(self, other_public: int) -> bytes  # Derive a 32-byte AES-256 key from the other side's public key.
    def format_init_message(self) -> str  # Format the CRYPTOINIT DH message for initiating key exchange.
    def format_reply_message(self) -> str  # Format the CRYPTOINIT DHREPLY message for completing key exchange.
    def parse_init_message(line: str) -> tuple  # Parse a CRYPTOINIT DH message into its components.
    def parse_reply_message(line: str) -> int  # Parse a CRYPTOINIT DHREPLY message to extract the public key.
  def encrypt(key: bytes, plaintext: bytes) -> bytes  # Encrypt plaintext using AES-256-GCM with a random IV.
  def decrypt(key: bytes, data: bytes) -> bytes  # Decrypt AES-256-GCM data produced by encrypt().
  def is_encrypted(data: bytes) -> bool  # Heuristic to detect whether a datagram contains encrypted or plaintext data.

── tmp/csc/clones/opus/upgrade_server-1773366886/repo/packages/csc-service/csc_service/shared/network.py ──
# Network communication module for the CSC shared package.
  class Network(Platform):
    def __init__(self, host='127.0.0.1', port=9525, name='network')  # Initializes the Network class.
    def _network_listener(self)  # Listens for all incoming data and filters keepalives.
    def start_listener(self)  # Starts the background network listener thread.
    def get_message(self)  # Gets one message tuple (bytes, addr) from the buffer.
    def send(self, message)  # Send a message to the server, encoding if necessary.
    def sock_send(self, data, addr)  # Sends data in chunks, handling both string and bytes types safely.
    def maybe_send_keepalive(self)  # Sends a periodic keep-alive packet silently.
    def close(self)  # Cleanly close the network socket and stop the listener thread.
    def connected_for(self)  # A placeholder method for subclasses to override.

    def machine()
    def processor()
    def system()
    def release()
    def version()
    def python_version()
  class Platform(Version):  # Extends Version with system capability detection and persistence.
    def __init__(self)
    def configure_install_mode(self, install_at_startup=False, install_as_needed=False)  # Configure install mode after construction (called by entry points).
    def _detect_all(self)  # Run all detection routines and populate self.platform_data.
    def _detect_hardware(self)  # Detect CPU, RAM, disk, architecture.
    def _detect_cpu_speed(self)  # Detect current/max CPU speed in MHz.
    def _detect_os(self)  # Detect OS type, version, distribution.
    def _detect_virtualization(self)  # Detect if running in a VM, container, WSL, or cloud.
    def _detect_geography(self)  # Detect timezone and locale-based geography.
    def _detect_time(self)  # Check system time accuracy.
    def _detect_network(self)  # Detect hostname and IP addresses safely using passive shell commands.

    def _detect_software(self)  # Detect installed software tools and their versions.

    def _detect_ai_agents(self)  # Detect which AI CLI tools are available in PATH.
    def _assess_resources(self)  # Assess whether this box can handle Docker + AI workloads.
    def _detect_runtime(self)  # Detect runtime directories (temp root, agent work base) in both Windows and Linux notations.
    def agent_temp_root(self)  # Get system temp directory path.
    def agent_work_base(self)  # Get CSC agent work base directory path.

    def logread(self, *args, filter_pattern=None) -> str  # LOGREAD <#chan> <logfile_path> [filter_pattern] - Read logfile to channel with optional filtering.

  class patch(Service):  # Fuzzy patch service for applying code changes to service modules.
    def __init__(self, server_instance)
    def _find_anchor(lines, line_hint, text_fragment)  # Find a line containing text_fragment near line_hint.
    def _parse_loose_patch(content)  # Parse loose anchor-format patch content.
    def _apply_hunks(lines, hunks)  # Apply parsed hunks to a list of lines.
    def _parse_unified_diff(content)  # Parse unified diff into hunks using fuzzy anchor matching.
    def _detect_format(content)  # Detect patch format. Returns 'loose', 'unified', or None.
    def _resolve_service_path(self, service_name)  # Convert a service name to its file path.
    def apply(self, patch_filename: str) -> str  # Version target files and apply a patch from patches/ directory.
    def _apply_content(self, content)  # Apply patch content (auto-detect format).
    def revert(self, service_name: str, version: str='latest') -> str  # Revert a service file to a previous version.
    def history(self, service_name: str) -> str  # Show version/patch history for a service file.
    def default(self, *args) -> str  # Show help and patch format examples.

# Queue Worker Service: Background service for processing agent prompt queues.
  class QueueWorkerService(Service):  # Background service for processing agent prompt queues.
    def __init__(self, server_instance)
    def _load_platform(self)  # Load platform data with dual-notation paths.
    def _ensure_agent_dirs(self, agent_name)  # Auto-create agent directories from template if they don't exist.
    def _ensure_temp_dirs(self)  # Auto-create temp/runtime directories if they don't exist.
    def _get_path_for_shell(self, windows_path, use_linux=False)  # Convert path to appropriate notation for shell (bash uses Linux notation).
    def is_process_alive(self, pid)  # Check if a process with given PID is still running.

    def add_verification_message(self, prompt_filename)  # Add verification message to incomplete workorder.
    def find_run_agent(self, agent_name)  # Find the run_agent script for an agent.
    def run_refresh_maps(self)  # Run refresh-maps to update code maps.
    def git_commit_push(self, message)  # Git add, commit, pull --rebase, and push changes.
    def spawn_agent(self, agent_name, prompt_filename)  # Start an agent using its run_agent script. Returns PID or None.
    def _any_agent_running(self)  # Check if any agent is currently running (has a .pid in queue/work/).
    def process_queue_in(self)  # Check agents/*/queue/in/ for new prompts and start agents.
    def process_queue_work(self)  # Check agents/*/queue/work/ for completed agents.
    def run_cycle(self)  # Run one complete queue processing cycle.
    def default(self, *args) -> str  # Show available commands.
    def cycle(self) -> str  # Run one queue processing cycle.
    def status(self) -> str  # Show queue status.

  class RunAgentExecutor:  # Execute an AI agent for a queued workorder.

    def execute(self) -> int  # Main execution flow: load, prepare, build, run, journal.

# Prompts Service - Work Queue Management
  class workorders(Service):  # Service for managing prompt files in a work queue system.

    def edit(self, *args) -> str  # Edit an existing prompt file.

    def assign(self, *args) -> str  # Assign a workorder to an agent with execution mode support.
    def move(self, *args) -> str  # Move a prompt between directories.
    def delete(self, filename: str) -> str  # Delete a prompt file.
    def status(self) -> str  # Show status of all prompt directories.
    def archive(self, filename: str) -> str  # Archive a completed prompt from done/ to archive/.
    def default(self, *args) -> str  # Show available commands.
  class prompts(workorders):  # Compatibility alias for legacy prompts service.

  class TestCurlServiceBasics(unittest.TestCase):  # Test basic curl service initialization and default behavior.
    def setUp(self)  # Set up mock server instance for each test.
    def test_init(self)  # Test curl service initializes correctly.
    def test_default_help(self)  # Test default command returns help message.
  class TestCurlServiceGetRequest(unittest.TestCase):  # Test GET request handling.
    def setUp(self)  # Set up mock server instance for each test.
    def test_simple_get_request(self, mock_get)  # Test simple GET request without headers.
    def test_get_with_single_header(self, mock_get)  # Test GET request with one custom header.
    def test_get_with_multiple_headers(self, mock_get)  # Test GET request with multiple custom headers.
    def test_get_header_whitespace_trimming(self, mock_get)  # Test that header keys and values have whitespace trimmed.
    def test_get_with_200_response(self, mock_get)  # Test successful 200 response.
    def test_get_with_201_response(self, mock_get)  # Test 201 Created response.
  class TestCurlServicePostRequest(unittest.TestCase):  # Test POST request handling.
    def setUp(self)  # Set up mock server instance for each test.
    def test_simple_post_request(self, mock_post)  # Test POST request with body data.
    def test_post_with_headers(self, mock_post)  # Test POST request with body and custom headers.
    def test_post_method_inferred_from_data(self, mock_post)  # Test that POST method is inferred when -d is present.
    def test_post_with_empty_body(self, mock_post)  # Test POST with empty data body.
    def test_post_with_special_characters(self, mock_post)  # Test POST with special characters in body.
    def test_post_notification_service(self, mock_post)  # Test real-world use case: ntfy.sh notification.
  class TestCurlServiceErrorHandling(unittest.TestCase):  # Test error handling and edge cases.
    def setUp(self)  # Set up mock server instance for each test.
    def test_no_url_specified(self)  # Test error when no URL is provided.
    def test_no_url_only_headers(self)  # Test error when headers provided but no URL.
    def test_no_url_only_data(self)  # Test error when data provided but no URL.
    def test_http_error_404(self, mock_get)  # Test HTTP 404 Not Found error handling.
    def test_http_error_500(self, mock_get)  # Test HTTP 500 Server Error handling.
    def test_connection_timeout(self, mock_get)  # Test connection timeout error handling.
    def test_invalid_url_from_requests(self, mock_get)  # Test invalid URL error from requests library.
    def test_connection_error_dns(self, mock_get)  # Test DNS resolution failure.
    def test_request_exception(self, mock_post)  # Test generic request exception handling.
    def test_malformed_header_caught_in_exception(self)  # Test that malformed headers (missing colon) are caught as error.
    def test_header_with_multiple_colons(self, mock_get)  # Test header value containing colons (e.g., URL).
  class TestCurlServiceTimeout(unittest.TestCase):  # Test timeout configuration and behavior.
    def setUp(self)  # Set up mock server instance for each test.
    def test_get_timeout_value(self, mock_get)  # Test that GET requests use 10 second timeout.

    def test_timeout_exception_handling(self, mock_get)  # Test handling of timeout exceptions.

    def setUp(self)  # Set up mock server instance for each test.
    def test_url_at_beginning(self, mock_get)  # Test URL at beginning with trailing headers.
    def test_flags_mixed_order(self, mock_get)  # Test that flag order doesn't matter.

# Tests for JulesMonitor service.
  class TestValidatePlan:
    def test_empty_plan_passes(self, monitor)  # Empty plan should have no issues.
    def test_multiple_classes_flagged(self, monitor)  # Plan with multiple classes in one file should flag issue.
    def test_single_class_ok(self, monitor)  # Single class in plan should not flag multi-class issue.
    def test_hardcoded_paths_flagged(self, monitor)  # Hardcoded /c/ paths without Platform() should flag.
    def test_platform_paths_ok(self, monitor)  # Using Platform() for paths should not flag.
    def test_missing_docstrings_flagged(self, monitor)  # Functions without docstrings should flag.
    def test_docstrings_ok(self, monitor)  # Functions with docstrings should not flag.
    def test_missing_type_hints_flagged(self, monitor)  # Functions without return type hints should flag.
    def test_type_hints_ok(self, monitor)  # Functions with type hints should not flag.
    def test_print_instead_of_log(self, monitor)  # Using print() without self.log() should flag.
    def test_log_usage_ok(self, monitor)  # Using self.log() alongside print() should not flag.
    def test_breaking_changes_flagged(self, monitor)  # Plan mentioning delete/remove without compat note should flag.
    def test_validation_has_timestamp(self, monitor)  # Validation result should include timestamp.
  class TestSessionHandling:
    def test_handle_awaiting_feedback_approve(self, monitor)  # Session with clean plan should auto-approve.
    def test_handle_awaiting_feedback_request_changes(self, monitor)  # Session with plan issues should request changes.
    def test_handle_awaiting_feedback_idempotent(self, monitor)  # Same session should not be processed twice.
    def test_handle_completion(self, monitor)  # Completed session should log PR URL and update tracking.
    def test_handle_completion_idempotent(self, monitor)  # Same completed session should not update twice.
    def test_handle_failure(self, monitor)  # Failed session should log error and update tracking.
    def test_monitor_execution_state_change(self, monitor)  # Execution monitoring should track state transitions.
  class TestAPIInteraction:
    def test_list_sessions(self, mock_requests, monitor)  # Should parse session list from API response.
    def test_list_sessions_error(self, mock_requests, monitor)  # Should return empty list on API error.
    def test_send_feedback_approve(self, mock_requests, monitor)  # Should send approval message.
    def test_send_feedback_reject(self, mock_requests, monitor)  # Should send rejection message with reason.
    def test_send_feedback_request_changes(self, mock_requests, monitor)  # Should send feedback with change requests.
  class TestRunCycle:
    def test_run_cycle_no_api_key(self, tmp_path)  # Should silently skip if no API key configured.
    def test_run_cycle_dispatches_states(self, mock_requests, monitor)  # Should dispatch to correct handler based on session state.

# Batch API Tool Loop Executor
  ESCALATION_CHAIN = ['claude-haiku-4-5-20251001', 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514', 'claude-opus-4-2025...
  DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES = 2
  LOGS_DIR = CSC_ROOT / 'logs'
  TOOLS = [{'name': 'read_file', 'description': 'Read the contents of a file', 'input_s...
  class BatchToolExecutor:  # Execute batch tool loops until completion with retry and escalation support.
    def __init__(self, api_key: str, max_retries: int=DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES)
    def verbose_log(self, msg: str, level: str='INFO')  # Log with CSC logging if available, store in execution log.
    def get_current_model(self) -> str  # Get the current model from escalation chain.
    def reset_for_phase(self)  # Reset retry/escalation state for a new phase.
    def should_escalate(self) -> bool  # Check if we should escalate to next model.
    def escalate_model(self) -> str  # Escalate to next model in chain. Returns new model name.
    def _normalize_path(self, path: str) -> str  # Convert Cygwin paths (/c/...) to Windows paths (C:\...) for Python file ops.
    def _try_alternative_paths(self, path: str) -> list  # Generate alternative paths to try if primary path fails.
    def execute_tool(self, name: str, input_data: dict) -> tuple[str, bool, float]  # Execute a tool and return (result_text, is_error, execution_time_ms).
    def wait_for_batch(self, batch_id: str, max_wait: int=3600) -> dict  # Poll batch status with exponential backoff until complete or timeout.
    def process_batch(self, batch_id: str, phase_name: str='') -> dict  # Process a single batch: retrieve results, execute tools, return state.
    def submit_followup_batch(self, custom_id: str, model: str, system_prompt: str, messages: list[dict], tool_results: list[dict]) -> str  # Submit a follow-up batch with tool results.
    def execute_batch_loop(self, initial_batch_id: str, phase_name: str='', max_iterations: int=20) -> dict  # Execute the full tool loop for a batch until end_turn.
    def verify_phase_success(self, result: dict) -> tuple[bool, str]  # Verify that a phase completed successfully.
    def execute_phase_with_retry(self, batch_id: str, phase_name: str, max_iterations: int=20, system_prompt: Optional[str]=None) -> dict  # Execute a phase with retry logic and model escalation.
  def get_api_key() -> str  # Extract API key from .env file or environment.
  def log(msg: str, level: str='INFO')  # Log message with timestamp.
  def get_model_short_name(model: str) -> str  # Get short display name for model.
  def main()

── tmp/csc/clones/opus/upgrade_server-1773367122/.trash/repo/packages/csc-service/csc_service/bridge/bridge.py ──
# Core bridge orchestrator -- bridges inbound clients to the CSC server.
  class Bridge:  # Bridges multiple inbound transports to an outbound transport.
    def __init__(self, inbound_transports: List[InboundTransport], outbound_transport: Optional[OutboundTransport], session_timeout: int=300, encrypt: bool=False, normalize_mode: Optional[str]=None, daemon_mode: bool=False)  # Initialize the bridge with transports and configuration.
    def start(self)  # Start all transports and background threads.
    def stop(self)  # Stop all transports, destroy all sessions, and clean up.
    def _on_client_data(self, data: bytes, client_id: ClientID, inbound: InboundTransport)  # Callback invoked by inbound transports when data arrives from a client.
    def _create_session(self, client_id: ClientID, inbound: InboundTransport) -> Optional[ClientSession]  # Create a new session for a client.
    def _handle_disconnect(self, client_id: ClientID)  # Handle a client disconnect event.
    def _destroy_session(self, session: ClientSession)  # Clean up and destroy a session, closing server connection gracefully.
    def _sniff_nick(self, session: ClientSession, data: bytes)  # Extract and store the nick from NICK commands in client data.
    def _forward_to_server(self, session: ClientSession, data: bytes)  # Forward client data to the CSC server with encryption and normalization.
    def _handle_control_command(self, session: ClientSession, data: bytes)  # Delegate control command handling to the session's control handler.
    def _forward_to_client(self, session: ClientSession, data: bytes)  # Forward server data back to the client with optional normalization.
    def _server_listener(self, session: ClientSession)  # Poll server for responses and forward to client (per-session thread).
    def _keepalive_loop(self)  # Send periodic keepalive PING to idle sessions (background thread).
    def _cleanup_loop(self)  # Remove sessions that have been idle longer than session_timeout.
    def session_count(self) -> int  # Return the number of active sessions.
    def list_sessions(self) -> List[dict]  # Return a list of summary dicts for all active sessions.
    def get_lobby_sessions(self) -> List[ClientSession]  # Return a list of all sessions currently in the LOBBY state.

  class ControlHandler:  # Handles IRC protocol logic for clients in the LOBBY state.

    def _send_raw(self, line)  # Send a raw IRC line to the client over the inbound transport.
    def _broadcast_lobby_message(self, message: str)  # Broadcast a chat message to all other clients in the lobby.
